title: Interpreting the Balance Sheet
description: Learn the differences between Assets and Liabilities, and how to calculate Total Equity. Understand Balance Sheet items like Cash, Accounts Receivable, and Deferred Revenue.

assessment_items.question_content:
Take a look at this balance sheet for The Great American Department Store.

**Based on the information available,
Total Assets = [[☃ input-number 1]]**










![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8cf546e97d2d2435e5706cb03044d181.jpg)
hints: Total Equity is the amount of Assets less Liabilities
Total Equity = Total Assets - Total Liabilities
Total Assets = Total Liabilities + Total Equity

options.alt: A balance sheet for the Great American Department Store for the period ending on July 31, 2012. The assets section has blank cells for cash and accounts receivable, and an X in the cell for total assets. The liabilities section has five hundred thousand dollars of accounts payable and five hundred thousand dollars of  of total liabilities. The total equity section has ninety-nine million five hundred fifty thousand dollars of common stock and ninety-nine million five hundred fifty thousand dollars of total equity.
